title: Google Workspace Organizational Units and Microsoft Groups

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The list and structure of the Organizational Units your agency has in your Google Workspace configuration, including sub-units. The easiest way to retrieve this is probably using GAM (https://github.com/GAM-team/GAM) by using the command "gam print orgs". Alternatively, it may be listed in the Google Admin console, but from my understanding it may be more difficult to retrieve the structure from that.

The list and structure of your Microsoft 365 Groups and Microsoft 365 Security Groups. This should be in the Microsoft 365 Admin Center and can be exported to a csv.

Personal student information may be excluded. If it makes things simpler, I do not need a comprehensive list of every single individual and accounts that are in the Organizational Units or Groups. However, if it's included in the output I would still appreciate any metadata such as shared and group email addresses, owner information, permission information, etc.

If you have any questions or wish to clarify any part of this request feel free to let me know.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Olivia

title: Anne "Annie" Doris Horne Missing Persons/Presumed Death

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Anne "Annie" Doris Horne Missing Persons/Presumed Death
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

regarding the missing persons case and presumed death of Anne Doris Horne of Richmond, Virginia, who went missing on June 3, 2001. I request the following files and will accept any that are available to me. I understand this case may be under investigation, I request any files that can be released to me. 
- Full and complete case file regarding Anne “Annie” Doris Horne
- Initial incident report - Any and all audio and video files related to the case
- Photographs or visual evidence related to the case
- Witness and family statements and interviews
- Field notes

I request a response time of five business days. I request these records be provided electronically, preferably by email attachment or PDF on a USB drive if email is not feasible. If access will take longer, please provide an estimated timeframe. As a member of the media pursuing this request for news-gathering purposes in the public interest, I request a waiver of all fees. If fees cannot be waived, please inform me if costs will exceed $250.

If you deny any portion, or all, of this request, please provide me with a written explanation of the reason(s) for your denial, including a citation to each specific statutory exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. If you conclude that portions of the records that I request are exempt from disclosure, please release the remainder of such records for inspection and copying, redacting only the portion or portions that you claim are exempt.

If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to reach out to char.adams@audiochuck.com or (463)-220-3997. Thank you for your time.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Courteney Stuart.
